folio.label: 176v
folio.l:
here obligacions and here bondes , by here oothes vp on here plg-
ges & borwes ,

and assigned he~ a certeyn day , to retourne
vn to his court ,

for to accepte & receyue the sentence & Iu-
gement , æ Melibe wolde comaunde to be doon on hem , by æe
causes a fore seid ,

whicú thynges ordeyned , euäy man retoåned
to his hous

¢ And whan æ dame Pèdence saw hire tyme , she
freyned & axed hire lord Melibe ,

what vengeau~ce he thougúte
to taken of hise aduäsaries

¢ To whicú , Melibe answered and
seide ¢ Certes q¶ he , I thynke & purpose me fully ,

to dishe-
rite he~ , of al æ euäe they han , & for to putte he~ in exile for
euere

¢ Certes q¶ Dame Pèdence , this were a cruel senten-
ce , and muchel a geyn resou~ ,

for ©e ben riche I now , and han
no nede , of othere me~nes good ,

& ©e mygút ligútly in this
wyse , gete ©ow a coueytous name ,

whicú is a vicious æi~gö
& ougúte ben eschewed of euäy good man ,

for after the sawe
of the word of thapostle ¢ Coueytise is rote of alle harmes

And æäfore it were bettere for ©ow , to lese muchel good of ©oåe
owene , than for to take of here good in this manere ,

for bet-
tere it is , to lese good witú worship~ , tha~ne it is , to wynne
good w vyleynye & shame ,

And euäy man ougúte to do his di-
ligence & his bysynesse to gete hi~ a good name ,

& ©et shal
he nat oonly , bisye hi~ , in kepynge his good name ,

but he
shal also enforcen hi~ alwey to do som thyngö , by whicú he
may renouelle his good name ,

For it is wryten ; æ æe olde
good loos , or good name of a man , is sone goon & passed ,
whan it is nat newed , ne renouelled

¢ And as touchynge
æ ©e seyn , ©e wol exile ©oure aduäsaries ,

æ thynketú me mu-
chel a geyn resou~ , & out of mesure ,

considered the power æ
they han ©euen ©ow , vp oon he~ self

¢ And it is wryten , æ
he is worthy to leese his péuylege , æ mys vsetú the mygút
& the power , æ is ©eue hi~

¢ And I sette cas , ©e mygúte
enioyne he~ æ peyne , by rigút & by lawe ,

whicú I trowe
©e mowe nat do ,

I seye ©e mygúte nat putte it to execu-
cion ¹auenture ,

And tha~ne were it likly to retoåne to the
werre , as it was biforn~ ,

And æäfore , if ©e wole æ men do
©ow obeisaunce , ©e muste deme more curteisly ,

this is to sey~ ;
©e muste ©eue more esy sentences & Iuggementz

¢ For it
is wryten , æ he that most curteisly comaundetú , to hi~ me~
muste obeyen

¢ And æäfore I preye ©ow , æ in this necessitee
& in this nede , ©e caste ©ow to ouäcome ©oure herte

¢ For Se-
nek seitú ; that he æ ouäcometú his herte , ouäcometú twyes ,

And Tullius seitú ; æäe is no thyngö so comendable in a g-
ret lord
as whan he is debonaire & meke , and appeisetú hi~
